county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Adams County formed 1867 from unorganized territory. County seat is Hastings. Located in south-central Nebraska along the Burlington and Missouri River Railroad line. German-Russian (Volga German) immigration was significant.
county_metadata.research_tips: Adams County (formed 1867, seat Hastings) records are held at the county courthouse in Hastings. The Adams County Historical Society maintains local genealogical materials including family files, photographs, and obituary indexes. History Nebraska (Nebraska State Historical Society) in Lincoln has microfilmed county records. The Hastings Museum holds regional history collections. German-Russian (Volga German) settlers were significant in the Hastings area -- check the American Historical Society of Germans from Russia (AHSGR) in Lincoln for immigration and church records. Burlington and Missouri River Railroad brought settlers to Adams County; railroad land sale records at Nebraska State Archives may document early purchasers. Federal census records (1870-1950) and the Nebraska state census of 1885 are available through FamilySearch and Ancestry. BLM GLO records at glorecords.blm.gov document original homestead and pre-emption entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Adams County. County Register of Deeds in Hastings holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Adams County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Adams County Register of Deeds, Hastings
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1867 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Adams County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1867. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1867. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Lincoln / Diocese of Grand Island / FamilySearch

source_profiles.repository: History Nebraska / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Adams County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, homestead proof notices, estate settlements, and community news. History Nebraska holds extensive microfilm of Nebraska newspapers. Chronicling America has digitized selections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Antelope County formed 1871 from the extinct L'Eau Qui Court County. County seat is Neligh. Named for antelope in the Elkhorn Valley. Northeast Nebraska agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Antelope County (formed 1871, seat Neligh) was created from the extinct L'Eau Qui Court County. Records are at the county courthouse in Neligh. Antelope County Historical Society in Neligh preserves local materials. Settlement was primarily by homesteaders in the 1870s-1880s; check BLM GLO records for homestead, timber culture, and pre-emption patents. The Elkhorn Valley along the Elkhorn River drew early settlers. German and Scandinavian immigrants were numerous; Lutheran and Catholic church records are important supplementary sources. FamilySearch has microfilmed Antelope County deed, probate, and marriage records. The 1885 Nebraska state census provides an important mid-decade snapshot. History Nebraska in Lincoln holds county records on microfilm and manuscript collections related to northeast Nebraska settlement.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Antelope County. County Register of Deeds in Neligh holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Arthur County formed 1887 from unorganized territory. County seat is Arthur. Sandhills cattle ranching county. One of Nebraska's least populated counties. Kinkaid Act (1904) homesteads.
county_metadata.research_tips: Arthur County (formed 1887, seat Arthur) is one of Nebraska's least-populated counties, located in the Sandhills. Records are at the small courthouse in Arthur. Due to the sparse population, record volumes are small and researchers may find limited local holdings. The Kinkaid Act (1904) was critical here, allowing 640-acre homesteads in the Sandhills -- check BLM GLO records and NARA for Kinkaid Act entries. Cattle ranching dominated the economy. History Nebraska in Lincoln may have supplementary materials. The 1885 Nebraska state census (if Arthur area was enumerated under a predecessor county) and federal censuses from 1890 (lost) and 1900 onward are the primary population records. FamilySearch and Ancestry have limited coverage for Arthur County due to its small population and late organization.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Arthur County. County Register of Deeds in Arthur holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Kinkaid Act Homestead Records -- Arthur County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kinkaid Act (1904) homestead records for Arthur County. The Kinkaid Act allowed 640-acre homestead claims in the Sandhills and other western Nebraska counties, recognizing that 160 acres was insufficient for ranching in semi-arid grasslands. Original case files at NARA; patents at BLM GLO.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Banner County formed 1888 from Cheyenne County. County seat is Harrisburg. Western Panhandle county. Very small population. Dry-land farming and ranching.
county_metadata.research_tips: Banner County (formed 1888, seat Harrisburg) in the western Panhandle was carved from Cheyenne County. Records are at the courthouse in Harrisburg. Another very small-population county with limited local genealogical infrastructure. Homestead and Timber Culture Act entries are key -- check BLM GLO records. The Union Pacific Railroad's southern line influenced settlement patterns. Scotts Bluff and Kimball county historical societies may have overlapping materials for this area. History Nebraska in Lincoln holds microfilm of available records. Federal censuses from 1890 (destroyed) and 1900 onward are available. The Panhandle's dry-land farming and ranching heritage means many families moved frequently, making land records especially important for tracking migration.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Banner County. County Register of Deeds in Harrisburg holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Blaine County formed 1885 from Custer County. County seat is Brewster. Central Sandhills county. Cattle ranching economy. Kinkaid Act (1904) homesteads important.
county_metadata.research_tips: Blaine County (formed 1885, seat Brewster) is a Sandhills county created from Custer County. Records are at the courthouse in Brewster. This is one of Nebraska's smallest counties by population. The Kinkaid Act (1904) drove much of the permanent settlement -- check BLM GLO records for 640-acre homestead entries. Cattle ranching is the primary industry. The Sandhills region has unique ecological and settlement history. History Nebraska in Lincoln holds what microfilm exists. FamilySearch coverage is limited. Researchers should also check neighboring Custer and Thomas county records for related families. Church records are scarce due to the sparse population and distances between settlements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Blaine County. County Register of Deeds in Brewster holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Boone County formed 1871 from unorganized territory. County seat is Albion. Notable Irish Catholic colony (O'Connor Colony) in the Albion area. Elkhorn Valley agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Boone County (formed 1871, seat Albion) records are at the courthouse in Albion. The Boone County Historical Society maintains local materials. Strong Irish Catholic settlement in the Albion and St. Edward areas -- the O'Connor Colony brought Irish families to Boone County in the 1870s. Catholic parish records (St. Michael's, Albion; St. Edward) are important sources for baptisms, marriages, and burials. Homestead entries dominated early land acquisition -- check BLM GLO records. The Union Pacific's Albion branch brought additional settlers. Czech/Bohemian families also settled in parts of Boone County. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 Nebraska state census is available. History Nebraska in Lincoln holds supplementary manuscript collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Boone County. County Register of Deeds in Albion holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Box Butte County formed 1886 from Dawes County. County seat is Alliance. Panhandle county. Alliance is a Burlington Railroad junction. Sugar beet farming later attracted diverse workers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Box Butte County (formed 1886, seat Alliance) was created from Dawes County in the Panhandle region. Records are at the courthouse in Alliance. Alliance was a major railroad junction (Burlington Railroad) and the town grew rapidly. The Box Butte County Historical Society preserves local materials. Homestead and pre-emption entries are key for early settlers -- check BLM GLO records. The Panhandle region attracted homesteaders from the eastern states and immigrants from Germany, Russia, and Scandinavia. Sugar beet farming brought Hispanic/Latino workers in the early 1900s. History Nebraska in Lincoln has microfilmed records. Alliance's Carhenge (modern) makes this a familiar name, but researchers should focus on the late 1880s-1910s settlement period for genealogical records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Box Butte County. County Register of Deeds in Alliance holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Boyd County formed 1891 from Holt County. County seat is Butte. One of the last Nebraska counties organized. Borders the Ponca Creek area with historical Native American connections.
county_metadata.research_tips: Boyd County (formed 1891, seat Butte) was created from Holt County and was one of the last Nebraska counties organized. Records are at the courthouse in Butte. Boyd County borders the Ponca Creek and Niobrara River areas with historical Native American connections. The county's late formation means earlier records may be in Holt County. Homestead entries are the primary land records -- check BLM GLO records. The 1890 federal census was destroyed by fire, making the 1900 census the first complete enumeration. German and Scandinavian settlers predominated. FamilySearch has some microfilmed records. History Nebraska in Lincoln is the best backup repository. Boyd County borders the former Ponca reservation area -- see Thurston County and NARA Kansas City for Ponca tribal records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Boyd County. County Register of Deeds in Butte holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brown County formed 1883 from unorganized territory. County seat is Ainsworth. North-central Nebraska. Transitional zone between Sandhills and northern valleys. Ranching and farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Brown County (formed 1883, seat Ainsworth) in north-central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Ainsworth. The Ainsworth area was served by the Fremont, Elkhorn and Missouri Valley Railroad, which influenced settlement patterns. Homestead and Kinkaid Act entries are important -- check BLM GLO records. Cattle ranching and farming coexist in this transitional zone between the Sandhills and the northern valleys. The Brown County Historical Society may have local materials. German and Scandinavian settlers were common. FamilySearch has limited microfilm for Brown County. History Nebraska in Lincoln holds supplementary materials. The 1885 Nebraska state census may include Brown County if it was organized by that date. Neighboring Keya Paha, Rock, and Cherry county records may contain related family information.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Brown County. County Register of Deeds in Ainsworth holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Brown County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Brown County Register of Deeds, Ainsworth
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1883 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Brown County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1883. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Kinkaid Act Homestead Records -- Brown County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kinkaid Act (1904) homestead records for Brown County. The Kinkaid Act allowed 640-acre homestead claims in the Sandhills and other western Nebraska counties, recognizing that 160 acres was insufficient for ranching in semi-arid grasslands. Original case files at NARA; patents at BLM GLO.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Buffalo County formed 1855 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Kearney. Platte Valley county on the Oregon, California, and Mormon Trails. Fort Kearny (1848-1871) was a key military post.
county_metadata.research_tips: Buffalo County (formed 1855, seat Kearney) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties and a key Platte Valley county. Records are at the courthouse in Kearney. The Buffalo County Historical Society in Kearney has extensive local collections. Kearney sits on the Oregon, California, and Mormon Trails -- trail-era records at History Nebraska and NARA may be relevant. Fort Kearny (1848-1871) military records are at NARA. The Union Pacific Railroad through Kearney brought settlers; railroad land grant records are important. Homestead entries are documented at BLM GLO. The University of Nebraska at Kearney archives hold regional history collections. German-Russian, Swedish, and Danish immigrants settled in the county. FamilySearch has microfilmed deed, probate, and marriage records. The 1885 state census is available. Kearney's newspapers from the 1870s are valuable for obituaries and legal notices.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Buffalo County. County Register of Deeds in Kearney holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Fort Kearny Records -- Buffalo County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Military records for Fort Kearny (1848-1871). Post returns, muster rolls, hospital records, and personnel files at NARA. History Nebraska may hold supplementary materials. Military post records document soldiers, dependents, civilian employees, and local interactions.

source_profiles.source_name: Union Pacific Railroad Land Records -- Buffalo County
source_profiles.repository: Union Pacific Historical Collection / History Nebraska / Nebraska State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Union Pacific Railroad land grant and sale records for Buffalo County. The UP received alternate sections of land along its right-of-way. Land department records document sales to settlers, town lot sales, and colony arrangements. UP Historical Collection at History Nebraska.
